methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $237M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

data.narrative:
Primer AI is a Series D-stage defense and security software company that has raised $237M to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer-median approach anchored to a comparable set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Saronic at $9.2B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B, and Epirus at $1.4B), supplemented by stage-progression methodology applied to total capital raised as a baseline proxy.

The stage-progression method implies a valuation of $356M, applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $237M raised baseline. The peer-median method yields $4.5B, reflecting the median valuation across the 3-company comparable set with a range spanning $1.4B to $9.2B. These two approaches triangulate to a low bound of $302M, a median estimate of $2.4B, and a high bound of $5.2B.

The wide dispersion between methods—spanning nearly 17x from low to high—reflects material uncertainty in stage-appropriate valuation multiples within the defense tech sector. The peer-median result is substantially elevated relative to raised capital, suggesting either that Primer has achieved outsized efficiency metrics versus peers, or that the peer set exhibits wide heterogeneity in investor conviction. Resolution of this spread requires granular analysis of unit economics, customer concentration, and revenue multiples relative to the named comparables.
